_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Complete staghorn stones associated with infundibular stenosis or distortion of intrarenal anatomy

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP reports

claim.label: Post procedure X Ray / USG KUB showing the stone removed

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure submitted?
Was the Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the Detailed Procedure/Operative/follow-up (For follow up visit) notes submitted?
Was the Post procedure X Ray / USG KUB showing the stone removed submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and IVP / NCCT/ CT-IVP not indicative of Nephrolithotomy procedure
Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the procedure missing or incomplete at pre-authorization stage
Intravenous pyelogram (IVP) / Non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT)/CT-IVP reports missing or incomplete at pre-authorization stage
Detailed Indoor case papers missing or incomplete at claim submission stage
Detailed Procedure / operation notes missing or incomplete at claim submission stage
Post procedure X Ray / USG KUB showing the stone removed missing or unclear at claim submission stage
Detailed discharge summary missing or incomplete at claim submission stage

clinical_key_pointers: Open: Open procedure indicated for large staghorn calculi. Access the kidney through an open approach, identifying a relatively avascular plane in the posterior kidney, and then making an incision through this plane and subsequently removing the calculus.
